ddls:
CREATE TABLE "contaminants" (
  "contaminant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contaminant */,
  "contaminant_name" TEXT /* Name of the contaminant */,
  "mcl_established" TEXT /* Whether a maximum contaminant level is established (Yes/No) */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the contaminant */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the contaminant and its potential effects */,
  "source" TEXT /* Common sources of the contaminant in water */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the contaminant information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contaminant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "water_samples" (
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each water sample */,
  "collection_date" TEXT /* Date when the sample was collected */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* ID of the site where the sample was collected */,
  "contaminant_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the contaminant being measured */,
  "collector_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data collector who collected the sample */,
  "sample_status" TEXT /* Status of the sample (e.g., collected, analyzed, archived) */,
  "collection_method" TEXT /* Method used to collect the sample */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the sample */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sample_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_water_samples_contaminant_id FOREIGN KEY ("contaminant_id") REFERENCES contaminants ("contaminant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contaminant_levels" (
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each level measurement */,
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* ID of the water sample associated with this level */,
  "highest_value" REAL /* Highest measured value of the contaminant */,
  "range_low" REAL /* Lower bound of the acceptable range */,
  "range_high" REAL /* Upper bound of the acceptable range */,
  "smcl" REAL /* Secondary maximum contaminant level */,
  "method_used" TEXT /* Method used to measure the contaminant level */,
  "certification_number" TEXT /* Certification number of the lab or equipment used */,
  "analyzed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the analyst who analyzed the sample */,
  "analysis_date" TEXT /* Date when the sample was analyzed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("level_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contaminant_levels_sample_id FOREIGN KEY ("sample_id") REFERENCES water_samples ("sample_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sites" (
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each site */,
  "site_name" TEXT /* Name of the site */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the site */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the site */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographical latitude of the site */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographical longitude of the site */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the site (e.g., treatment plant, reservoir, well) */,
  "last_inspected" TEXT /* Date of the last inspection of the site */,
  "inspector_id" INTEGER /* ID of the inspector who last inspected the site */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "level_id" INTEGER /* ID of the contaminant level that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was generated */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message detailing the nature of the alert */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolved" TEXT /* Whether the alert has been resolved (Yes/No) */,
  "resolution_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  "follow_up_actions" TEXT /* Actions taken to resolve the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("level_id") REFERENCES contaminant_levels ("level_id")
)
